text: When writing code, encountering bugs is inevitable. Identifying bugs requires patience; you need to reproduce the issue and observe unexpected behavior. A systematic approach helps: start by isolating the problematic section, then test each part step by step. Modern debugging tools are invaluable—they let you pause execution, inspect variables, and trace errors. Logging is another powerful strategy, as it records program flow and variable values, making it easier to spot where things go wrong. By combining these methods, you can debug efficiently and avoid guesswork.
